clinical_history: What is this wide complex tachycardia in an 88 year old woman?
diagnosis:
This ECG shows monomorphic ventricular tachycardia at a rate of about 140 bpm with a very wide (QRS duration at least 180 msec) with a right bundle branch block and superior axis deviation morphology; (for comparison, see also Case # 274). Note the broad RS in lead III. The QRS also has a wide notched R wave in V1 and V2. 
The previous ECG done on this patient the same day showed an extensive anterolateral ST elevation acute myocardial infarction (see Case # 289). There is definite suggestion of superimposed marked ST elevation in V5 and V6, approaching a monophasic current of injury ("tombstone pattern") which makes the QRS appear even wider than it is. The presence of monomorphic VT in acute coronary disease strongly suggests an underlying scar.
